title: ruppia maritima subsp. spiralis
intro: Ruppia maritima subsp. spiralis, commonly known as spiral-waved sea lavender or spiral-waved ruppia, is a submersed aquatic plant species that belongs to the family Ruppiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds and skin infections. The health benefits of Ruppia maritima subsp. spiralis include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in water or using it as a tincture, although further research is needed to confirm its safety and efficacy for human consumption.
